title: Tender Fluffy Middle Eastern Cornmeal Maqluba
description: A traditional Middle Eastern dish featuring white cornmeal, served in a lotus leaf wrap, perfect for a crowd-pleasing dinner.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine cornmeal, flour, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, mix together ginger, olive oil, and water.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
If using rose water, stir it in at this point.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 5 minutes, until smooth.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each into a ball.
Place a ball of dough on the center of a lotus leaf and flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Place another lotus leaf on top and press the edges to seal the packet.
Repeat with the remaining dough and leaves, forming 8 packets in total.
Place the packets seam-side down on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 30 minutes, or until the cornmeal is golden brown and the packets are puffed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped parsley.
Let the cornmeal cool in the packets for at least 10 minutes before unwrapping and serving.
Once cooled, the cornmeal is ready to be served and enjoyed!
